name: Barranco del Cabrerizo y Mirador de Peñas Royas – running circular through the Pinares de Rodeno
name_original: Barranco del Cabrerizo y Mirador de Peñas Royas – running circular por los Pinares de Rodeno
region_intro: Jogging routes around Albarracín traverse a landscape characterized by red sandstone formations, dense pine forests, and the winding Guadalaviar River. The region is situated within the Sierra de Albarracín mountains, offering varied terrain from riverside paths to elevated trails along medieval walls. Runners can experience a mix of natural features and historical elements throughout the area. Best jogging routes around Albarracín The most popular jogging route is Barranco del Cabrerizo y Mirador de Peñas Royas – running circular through the Pinares de Rodeno, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 27 minutes to complete, leading through the distinctive red sandstone landscape of Pinares de Rodeno. Another top favourite among local runners is Dornaque to Peña del Hierro Viewpoint Trail, an easy 2.9 miles (4.6 km) path offering views from Peña del Hierro. Local runners also love the Lázaro Shelter loop from Albarracín, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail leading through forested areas, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Jogging around Albarracín is defined by red sandstone cliffs, pine forests, and riverside paths along the Guadalaviar River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Albarracín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Albarracín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Albarracín?
A: There are over 15 dedicated running routes around Albarracín, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. The komoot community has explored these trails over 500 times, giving them an average rating of 5.0 stars.

Q: Are there any easy jogging trails suitable for beginners in Albarracín?
A: Yes, Albarracín offers several easy jogging trails perfect for beginners. One excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983280110/dornaque-to-pena-del-hierro-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dornaque to Peña del Hierro Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.9-mile (4.6 km) path that provides beautiful views from Peña del Hierro without significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Albarracín area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Albarracín are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983266372/barranco-del-cabrerizo-y-mirador-de-penas-royas-running-circular-through-the-pinares-de-rodeno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cabrerizo y Mirador de Peñas Royas – running circular through the Pinares de Rodeno&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.0-mile (11.3 km) trail that takes you through the distinctive red sandstone landscape.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while running in Albarracín?
A: Jogging in Albarracín offers a diverse natural landscape. You'll encounter unique red sandstone formations, lush pine forests, and scenic paths along the winding Guadalaviar River. The region is part of the Sierra de Albarracín mountains, providing varied terrain and stunning vistas, especially within the Pinares de Rodeno Natural Park.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running trails?
A: Yes, Albarracín's rich history is intertwined with its natural beauty. You can run alongside the town's ancient medieval walls and towers, offering panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/689101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albarracín Old Town&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3978244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Town of Albarracín&lt;/a&gt; themselves are highlights. You might also spot the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4543098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doña Blanca Tower&lt;/a&gt; or even ancient cave paintings within the Cultural Park of Albarracín.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: While many routes focus on the river and forests, the wider Sierra de Albarracín region is known for its water sources. You can find beautiful waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/604533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Mill Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; nearby, which can be incorporated into longer runs or visited as part of a combined outdoor experience.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Albarracín?
A: The running trails in Albarracín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique red sandstone landscapes, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the scenic riverside paths along the Guadalaviar River. The mix of natural beauty and historical elements also makes for a memorable experience.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Albarracín offers several challenging routes for experienced runners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31447656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lázaro Shelter loop from Albarracín&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.3-mile (10.2 km) trail with significant elevation gain, leading through forested areas and providing a good workout. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31447834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Fraile loop from Albarracín&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Albarracín?
A: Albarracín offers great running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with mild temperatures and vibrant colors in the landscape. Summers can be warm, but many routes through the pine forests offer welcome shade. The crisp mountain air makes for an invigorating experience in any season.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Yes, the region is dotted with numerous viewpoints that offer breathtaking vistas. Trails often lead to or pass by spots like Mirador del Cabrerizo, Mirador de Peñas Royas, and Mirador Peña El Hierro. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983280110/dornaque-to-pena-del-hierro-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dornaque to Peña del Hierro Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase these stunning views.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Albarracín?
A: Many of the natural trails and paths in the Sierra de Albarracín are suitable for running with dogs, especially those through the pine forests and along the river. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in protected areas like the Pinares de Rodeno Natural Park, and to be mindful of local regulations and other trail users.

Q: Are there family-friendly running options in Albarracín?
A: For families, the easier and shorter routes are ideal. Paths along the Guadalaviar River, with their varied terrain including stone paths and suspension bridges, can be an engaging option. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983280110/dornaque-to-pena-del-hierro-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dornaque to Peña del Hierro Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a good choice due to its easy difficulty and manageable distance.
tips: The Peñas Royas Viewpoint is one of the most spectacular spots in the natural surroundings of Albarracín, located within the Pinares de Rodeno Natural Park in the province of Teruel. It is located just a few kilometers from Albarracín's historic center.
Mirador del Cabrerizo is located near Abrigo de la Fuente del Cabrerizo.
